gaps.requirement: FR-BC1 + FR-BC2 + FR-BC5 (with FR-BA2 insert technique)
gaps.quote: Every delivery MUST run as a single canary transaction: resolve the target compose node → verify it is writable (not merely present/attached) → insert via a native-setter + dispatched InputEvent (never a naive .value = / .textContent = assignment, which does not register with the app's editor model) → read back the result within a ~250 ms budget → branch to success or one of four failure classes.
gaps.why_uncovered: E3-S1/S2/S3 build the injection framework + per-provider selectors and E5-S2 builds a heartbeat canary, but no story owns the delivery-transaction discipline itself: verify-writable-before-visible, the native-setter+dispatched-InputEvent technique (guards the named 'injected-but-wrong' counter-metric / supermemory missing-dispatch failure), the read-back assertion (FR-BC2), and the four-class branch NOTFOUND/NOTWRITABLE/AMBIGUOUS/VERIFY each alerted separately (FR-BC5). E3-S2 has only one failure mode (INJECTION_TARGET_MISSING); E5-S2 has a single broken(provider,selector) state.

proposed_issue.title: [extension] Canary delivery-transaction — resolve→verify-writable→native-setter+InputEvent insert→read-back(~250ms)→four-class branch
proposed_issue.milestone: B3 — Per-provider delivery adapters — injection framework, ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude/Grok posture
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery engineer** I want **every injection to run as one atomic canary transaction — resolve the compose node, verify it is writable, insert via a native value-setter plus a dispatched `InputEvent`, read the result back within a ~250 ms budget, then branch to success or one of four typed failure classes** so that **a write that never registers with the provider's editor model (the supermemory-style missing-dispatch failure named as a counter-metric) can never silently pass as delivered**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestDeliveryRunsAsCanaryTransactionWithReadBackAndFourClasses` (integration against a provider-shaped DOM fixture — a native-setter+`InputEvent` write registers with the editor model and read-back confirms it within ~250 ms; a disabled node yields `NOTWRITABLE`; a multi-match yields `AMBIGUOUS`; a write that does not register yields `VERIFY`; a missing node yields `NOTFOUND`; a naive `.value=` write is proven to fail read-back). *Exact writer technique + budget + class enum are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a resolved writable compose node, When insertion runs, Then it uses a native value-setter + dispatched `InputEvent` and read-back confirms the text landed **and** registered with the editor model within ~250 ms. *Assert: editor-model value == prompt within budget.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC1, FR-BC2]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a naive `.value=`/`.textContent=` write, When read-back runs, Then it is detected as non-registering and classified `VERIFY`, never reported success. *Assert: naive write → VERIFY, not done.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC1, FR-BC2; §2 counter-metric "Injected-but-wrong"]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given each failure mode, When the transaction branches, Then it emits exactly one of NOTFOUND / NOTWRITABLE / AMBIGUOUS / VERIFY, each carrying provider + selector-version and alerted separately. *Assert: enum ⊆ {4 classes}; class matches the induced fault.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC5]
- [ ] **AC4 (nothing-visible-on-abort)** — Given resolve or verify-writable fails, When it aborts, Then nothing reaches the visible compose surface and it hands off to the FR-BC4 fallback. *Assert: no DOM mutation on abort; fallback signalled.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC1, FR-BC3]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestDeliveryRunsAsCanaryTransactionWithReadBackAndFourClasses` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/adapters/framework` transaction runner (resolve → verify-writable → native-setter+`InputEvent` → read-back → 4-class branch), pattern-donated from `mem0ai/mem0-chrome-extension` (FR-BA2), consumed by every E3 adapter.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/adapters/framework` (the E3-S1 `InjectionPort` internals). The PRD is explicit that **the canary IS the delivery transaction, not a separate check** — E5-S2's heartbeat watches selectors, this owns the per-delivery write discipline the heartbeat classes ride on. The `InputEvent`-dispatch technique is the mem0 pattern donation (FR-BA2) that supermemory's own writers omit.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestDeliveryRunsAsCanaryTransactionWithReadBackAndFourClasses` (integration, real DOM fixture). Tiers: unit (class branch) + integration (native-setter write + read-back). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real DOM, never a mocked writer.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/5/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (fail-typed not silent, read-back is the proof, no naive assignment).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-E (FR-BC1/BC2/BC5), F-G FR-BA2 (mem0 composer-write donation); build-vs-buy `IgOjzk034v`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2717** (E3-S1 injection port), **ENG-2690** (Definition Pack — writer technique + class enum + contract TAG).
- [ ] Blocks: E3-S2/S3 adapters (they run through this transaction), E5-S2 canary (classes), E5-S3 alert.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery maintainer** I want **per-provider selector/DOM contracts to ship as versioned JSON fetched and signature-verified at runtime — data, not code — so a broken selector is patched within a bounded canary-trip-to-fix SLA** so that **a provider UI change is repaired in hours over the data channel, not in store-review days, without ever shipping remote code (FR-TS4 intact)**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestSelectorDataFetchedSignedVersionedAndSLABounded` (integration — the extension fetches a versioned selector-data document, rejects an unsigned/invalid-signature or version-regressed document fail-closed, adopts a valid newer version without a code change, and a measured canary-trip→adopted-fix path is instrumented against the SLA). *Exact channel URL, signing scheme, and SLA number are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a signed newer selector-data version, When fetched, Then the extension validates the signature, adopts it, and resolves the new selectors with no code change. *Assert: post-fetch resolution uses new data; zero bundle change.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC7]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given an unsigned, tampered, or version-regressed document, When fetched, Then it is rejected fail-closed and the last-good version stays in force. *Assert: bad doc → rejected; resolution unchanged.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC7; F-F FR-TS4 (no remote code — data only)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a canary trip, When a fixed selector-data version is published, Then the trip-to-adopted-fix duration is measured against the ≤24h SLA (NFR-8). *Assert: SLA timer emitted canary-trip-to-fix.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-8; §5 F-E FR-BC7]
- [ ] **AC4 (no-remote-code)** — Given the fetched payload, When evaluated, Then it is pure selector data and can never carry executable logic. *Assert: schema forbids code; a planted script field → RED.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-F FR-TS4]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestSelectorDataFetchedSignedVersionedAndSLABounded` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/adapters/dataplane` (fetch + signature-verify + version-monotonic adopt + last-good fallback), SLA instrumentation feeding NFR-8, schema guard forbidding code fields.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/adapters/dataplane`. This is the durability spine NFR-8 leans on and the transport the FR-PA3 tier-config also rides. E5-S2 owns the canary + local fixtures; this owns the remote data channel that makes a fix a data-push, not a store release.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestSelectorDataFetchedSignedVersionedAndSLABounded` (integration). Tiers: unit (signature + version monotonicity) + integration (fetch→adopt→resolve). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real fetch against a fixture channel; never mock the verifier.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (fail-closed on bad data, signed supply chain, data-not-code, no silent stale).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-E FR-BC7, §6 NFR-8; F-F FR-TS4; §8 store-distribution posture (latency risk owned by this channel, not store review).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2725** (E5-S2 canary + fixtures), **ENG-2690** (channel URL + signing + SLA + contract TAG).
- [ ] Blocks: the FR-PA3 tier-config story (rides this channel), E5-S3 fast-fix loop.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **product owner** I want **each provider's GO/DEGRADE tier driven by a signed remote config value — not hard-coded — so promoting Claude or Grok to GO (or demoting a GO) is a data change over the FR-BC7 channel** so that **the viability verdict's 'route to counsel' outcome and the post-build shipping clearance are honored by data, not a code release**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestProviderTierIsConfigDrivenNotCodeRewrite` (integration — a signed tier-config flips a provider DEGRADE→GO and the shipped delivery path changes accordingly with no code change; an absent/invalid tier value fails closed to DEGRADE; injection stays default-off for a provider until its tier config explicitly enables it). *Exact tier-config schema is pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a signed tier-config promoting Claude DEGRADE→GO, When adopted, Then Claude delivery switches to injection with no code change. *Assert: post-config path == inject; zero bundle change.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA3]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a missing or invalid tier value for a provider, When resolved, Then the provider fails closed to DEGRADE (copy/paste), never open to injection. *Assert: unknown tier → DEGRADE.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA3; §5 F-B FR-PA1 (conservative default)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the counsel shipping gate, When a GO provider (ChatGPT/Gemini) has not been cleared, Then its injection stays default-off until the tier config enables it. *Assert: uncleared GO → no default-on injection.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU Counsel decision 2026-07-15 (SHIPPING not cleared); §5 F-B FR-PA4]
- [ ] **AC4 (data-not-code)** — Given the tier config, When transported, Then it rides the FR-BC7 signed remote-JSON channel and carries no executable logic. *Assert: shares the FR-BC7 verifier; schema forbids code.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA3; F-F FR-TS4]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestProviderTierIsConfigDrivenNotCodeRewrite` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/registry/tier` reading a signed tier value over the FR-BC7 channel, fail-closed-to-DEGRADE default, default-off-until-cleared gate; E3-S4 posture matrix consumes it instead of a hard-coded tier.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/registry/tier`, feeding the E3-S4 posture matrix and E2-S3 status. The verdict `V6hlDjecfh` leaves Claude/Grok DEGRADE **contested** and routes to counsel; the 2026-07-15 counsel addendum clears BUILD but not SHIPPING — both outcomes require tier to be a data knob, not a rebuild.

**⚠️ Dedupe note (added on verification pass):** the signed remote-JSON channel this story reads tier values FROM overlaps with gap-hunt/ext-sweep/verdict.json's already-CONFIRMED '[extension] Remote versioned selector + tier/posture-config pack channel' proposal (same milestone boundary, ships selectors AND tier/posture config together). Do not re-build the fetch/schema/signature/version mechanism here — this story's non-overlapping scope is the CONSUMPTION semantics: fail-closed-to-DEGRADE on a missing/invalid tier value (AC2) and the default-off-until-cleared shipping gate (AC3). A human should merge the channel-plumbing GREEN tasks into the verdict.json ticket and keep only the registry/consumption logic here.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestProviderTierIsConfigDrivenNotCodeRewrite` (integration). Tiers: unit (tier resolution + fail-closed) + integration (config flip changes delivery path). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real config over the fixture channel.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (conservative fail-closed default, data-driven policy, no baked tier).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-B FR-PA3/PA1/PA4, Counsel decision (2026-07-15); verdict `V6hlDjecfh`; FR-BC7 channel.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2720** (E3-S4 posture matrix), **ENG-2690** (tier-config schema + TAG); needs the FR-BC7 remote-data-channel story.
- [ ] Blocks: any Claude/Grok DEGRADE→GO re-tier; the shipping default-off gate.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery engineer** I want **the extension to detect at runtime when a provider's composer root is a `mode:'closed'` shadow root and treat that as a categorical DOM-injection impossibility that demotes the provider to DEGRADE via the tier-config path** so that **a structurally-unreachable composer (most plausibly Gemini's Angular/Material surface) falls cleanly to copy/paste instead of endlessly canary-tripping as if it were a fixable selector**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestClosedShadowRootForcesCategoricalDegrade` (integration against a fixture whose composer root is an attached closed shadow root — the extension classifies it categorically-unreachable, demotes the provider to DEGRADE through the FR-PA3 tier path, and does not emit a repeatable canary-selector trip; an open/light-DOM composer stays GO). *Exact detection contract is pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a composer root that is a `mode:'closed'` shadow root, When delivery is attempted, Then the provider is categorically classified DOM-unreachable and demoted to DEGRADE. *Assert: closed root → DEGRADE, distinct class from NOTFOUND.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA6]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the demotion, When routed, Then it flows through the FR-PA3 tier-config path, not a canary selector trip that pages the team repeatedly. *Assert: no per-attempt NOTFOUND storm; tier == DEGRADE.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA6, FR-PA3]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a light-DOM `contenteditable` composer (e.g. Gemini Quill/Angular per addendum §A), When checked, Then it is NOT falsely demoted — the gate rules the hypothesis out. *Assert: light-DOM composer stays GO.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA6 (calibration: name the gate, do not pre-judge)]
- [ ] **AC4 (ToS-independent)** — Given a provider with a ToS GO, When its composer root is closed, Then it still falls to DEGRADE regardless of ToS. *Assert: closed root overrides ToS-GO.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA6; OQ-E2]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestClosedShadowRootForcesCategoricalDegrade` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/adapters/shadow-gate` (closed-shadow-root probe), demotion via the FR-PA3 tier path, wiring into the Gemini adapter (E3-S3).

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/adapters/shadow-gate`. The §7 precondition-3 live-DOM prototype (spike lane) rules the hypothesis in/out pre-build; this is the shipped runtime guard that makes the outcome durable and distinct from the canary. Addendum §A lowers the prior for Gemini (Quill light-DOM `contenteditable`) but the PRD insists the gate be named, not pre-judged.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestClosedShadowRootForcesCategoricalDegrade` (integration, closed-root fixture). Tiers: unit (root-mode probe) + integration (demotion path). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real shadow-root fixture.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (categorical vs transient, fail-safe to copy/paste, no team-alert storm).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-B FR-PA6/PA3, §7 precondition 3, §8 OQ-E2, addendum §A.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2719** (E3-S3 Gemini adapter), **ENG-2690** (detection contract + TAG); needs the FR-PA3 tier-config story.
- [ ] Blocks: Gemini GO ship decision, E2-S3 honest status.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

Distinct from the sibling ext-sweep items: addendum.json's REFUTED item and verdict.json's CONFIRMED 'Live-DOM canary validation' item are both a one-time PRE-GA validation/spike that records a GO/DEGRADE verdict once before ship; this proposed_issue is the shipped PRODUCTION runtime guard (`src/adapters/shadow-gate`) that classifies and demotes on every live delivery going forward, not a one-off gate — the two are complementary (the validation gate proves the runtime code is needed; the runtime code is what actually enforces it in the field), not duplicative. proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery maintainer** I want **the canary's selectors expressed as a multi-candidate manifest of locale-invariant anchors (`data-testid`/`role`/structural) that never key on localized `aria-label`/placeholder/visible text** so that **an EU-beachhead French or German provider DOM, or a per-region A/B UI variant, does not false-trip the canary and erode trust in the fail-loud signal**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestCanaryToleratesLocaleAndABVariantsNoFalseTrip` (integration — across French/German/English and two A/B-variant fixtures of the same provider the canary stays green via the multi-candidate manifest; a static lint proves no selector keys on a localized ARIA/placeholder/visible-text string; a genuine structural change still trips). *Exact locale/variant fixture set is pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given French, German, and English fixtures of the same healthy composer, When the canary runs, Then it stays green in every locale. *Assert: all locales → green.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC6]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a selector manifest, When statically linted, Then no selector keys on a localized `aria-label`/placeholder/visible-text string; only locale-invariant anchors are allowed. *Assert: planted localized-text selector → RED.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC6]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given two per-region A/B UI variants, When resolved, Then the multi-candidate manifest matches both without a false trip. *Assert: both variants → resolved, no alert.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC6; §2 counter-metric (false-positive breakage)]
- [ ] **AC4 (still-loud)** — Given a genuine structural composer change, When the canary runs, Then it still trips (no silent degrade), logged with provider+selector-version. *Assert: real change → trip logged.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC6]

## 🔨 Tasks

## 🎯 Story

As a **user mid-draft in the assistant** I want **injection to detect text I have already typed and never silently overwrite it — inserting at my cursor / appending, or asking replace/append/cancel — and to never yank my cursor focus without an announced, predictable transition** so that **the extension augments my compose box without destroying my work or disorienting a keyboard/screen-reader user**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestInjectionPreservesDraftAndDoesNotStealFocus` (integration — with existing non-empty content the injection inserts-at-cursor/appends or surfaces a replace/append/cancel choice and never clobbers; a screen-reader/keyboard trace shows any focus move is announced and predictable, never a silent steal). *Exact draft-collision UX + focus contract are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a compose node with an in-progress user draft, When injection runs, Then it does not silently overwrite it — it inserts-at-cursor/appends or prompts replace/append/cancel. *Assert: prior draft text still present unless the user chose replace.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC8]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the user chooses cancel, When resolved, Then nothing is injected and the draft is untouched. *Assert: cancel → zero mutation.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC8]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given injection completes, When focus is traced, Then any focus transition is announced and predictable — focus is never silently stolen from the user's cursor. *Assert: focus move announced; no un-announced steal.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-10]
- [ ] **AC4 (empty-composer)** — Given an empty compose node, When injection runs, Then it writes directly with no collision prompt. *Assert: empty → direct insert, no dialog.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC8]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestInjectionPreservesDraftAndDoesNotStealFocus` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: pre-insert non-empty-content detection, insert-at-cursor/append + replace/append/cancel affordance, announced focus-transition helper in the injection port.

## 🎯 Story

As a **privacy-conscious user** I want **the consent gate to tell me plainly that anything injected into the assistant or placed on my clipboard leaves Orvex's deletion reach and is readable by other apps/extensions/OS clipboard history, and for the clipboard fallback to auto-clear and to flag employer-scoped copies** so that **I consent to the real exposure, and my clipboard is not a lingering leak of private or employer memory**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestDeletionBoundaryDisclosedAndClipboardHygiene` (integration — the consent panel discloses the injection/clipboard deletion boundary before delivery; a copy/paste fallback auto-clears the clipboard payload after the timeout or on next copy; employer-scoped copied content is visibly flagged; the same consent gate covers the fallback path). *Exact disclosure copy + auto-clear timeout are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given any delivery, When consent is requested, Then the panel discloses that injected/clipboard content is outside Orvex's later deletion reach and readable by other apps/extensions/OS clipboard sync. *Assert: disclosure shown pre-delivery, not only at abort.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF3; F-E FR-BC4(a)]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a copy/paste fallback payload, When the timeout elapses or the next copy occurs, Then the clipboard payload is auto-cleared. *Assert: clipboard cleared at timeout/next-copy.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC4(b)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given copied content includes employer-scoped memory, When shown, Then it is visibly flagged. *Assert: employer-scoped copy → visible flag.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-E FR-BC4(c); F-D FR-CF3]
- [ ] **AC4 (fallback-gated)** — Given the clipboard fallback path, When it runs, Then the FR-CF1 consent gate has already run — the fallback is not an unconsented neutral path. *Assert: unconsented private memory absent from clipboard.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF1; F-E FR-BC4(a)]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestDeletionBoundaryDisclosedAndClipboardHygiene` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: deletion-boundary disclosure in the consent panel, clipboard auto-clear timer + next-copy clear, employer-scope flag, fallback→consent-gate ordering.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/consent/disclosure` + `src/fallback/copypaste` hygiene. Hardens E4-S1 (consent) and E5-S1 (fallback) with the honesty the PRD refuses to assume away — the clipboard is not a neutral, unconsented path.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (disclose real exposure, minimize clipboard lifetime, fail-safe consent order).

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-D FR-CF1/CF3, F-E FR-BC4; FR-L5 deletion boundary (inherited).

## 🎯 Story

As a **user removing the extension or disconnecting an assistant** I want **all locally stored connection state, cached memory bundles, and the consent/injection audit log wiped, and the audit log bounded and protected while it lives** so that **uninstall/disconnect leaves no residual memory on the device and the trust-evidence log cannot itself become a hoard of private content**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestUninstallAndDisconnectWipeAllAndAuditLogIsBounded` (integration — disconnect and the uninstall hook clear connection state, cached memory bundles, and the audit log; the audit log enforces a retention window + size cap, stores no memory content beyond breakage/consent evidence, and is protected at rest). *Exact retention window/size cap + at-rest protection are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given an explicit disconnect, When it runs, Then connection state, cached memory bundles, and the consent/injection audit log for that assistant are cleared. *Assert: post-disconnect stores empty for that assistant.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF6]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given uninstall, When the uninstall hook fires, Then all locally stored state is wiped. *Assert: no residual bundle/log/token after uninstall.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF6; §6 NFR-6]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the audit log, When it grows, Then it is bounded by a retention window and size cap and stores no memory content beyond breakage/consent evidence. *Assert: over-window/over-cap entries evicted; no full memory bodies stored.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF6; §6 NFR-6]
- [ ] **AC4 (at-rest)** — Given the audit log at rest, When inspected, Then it is protected (not world-readable plaintext of sensitive content). *Assert: at-rest protection applied.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF6]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestUninstallAndDisconnectWipeAllAndAuditLogIsBounded` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: disconnect + `runtime.onInstalled`/uninstall wipe of state/bundles/log, audit-log ring with retention window + size cap, at-rest protection, evidence-only redaction.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/storage/lifecycle` + `src/obs/audit`. Extends E2-S2 (token revoke) to the bundle/audit-log surface and gives NFR-6's trust-evidence log a bounded, protected lifecycle. The audit log is written by E4-S1; this owns its retention and wipe.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

Named DoD `TestUninstallAndDisconnectWipeAllAndAuditLogIsBounded` (integration). Tiers: unit (retention/size eviction, redaction) + integration (disconnect/uninstall wipe). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real extension storage in a headless context.

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (wipe-on-uninstall, bounded auditable log, protect at rest, minimal retained content).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-D FR-CF6, §6 NFR-6.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2721** (E4-S1 consent/audit record), **ENG-2715** (E2-S2 storage), **ENG-2690** (retention/at-rest contract + TAG).

## 🎯 Story

As a **user with separate personal and employer identities** I want **the extension to verify the provider tab's signed-in account matches the scope of the memory it is about to inject — reading an account/workspace signal where one exists, else asking me to confirm the target account — and to fail closed to copy/paste on any mismatch, mid-session account switch, or undetectable target** so that **employer memory can never land in a personal account (or vice versa) because I was signed into the wrong identity**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestTargetIdentityMatchesScopeOrFailsClosed` (integration — a scope-matched target injects; a detected account mismatch, a mid-session account switch, and an undetectable target each fail closed to copy/paste with no silent injection; where no reliable signal exists, an explicit target-account confirmation is required before injecting scoped memory). *Exact per-provider identity-signal set is pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a provider surface that exposes an account/workspace signal, When scoped memory is delivered, Then the extension verifies the signed-in identity matches the memory's scope before injecting. *Assert: mismatch detected pre-injection.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF7]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given no reliable identity signal, When scoped memory is delivered, Then the extension requires explicit user confirmation of the target account before injecting. *Assert: no confirm → no scoped injection.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF7]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a detected mismatch, a mid-session account switch, or an undetectable target, When delivery runs, Then it fails closed to copy/paste, never silent injection. *Assert: each case → fallback, zero injection.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF7]
- [ ] **AC4 (own-metadata)** — Given the scope decision, When made, Then WHICH memory is still chosen from Orvex metadata (FR-CF2) — this story adds only the WHICH-identity check, without scraping conversation content. *Assert: no page-content read beyond the identity signal.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-D FR-CF7, FR-CF2, F-A FR-D5]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestTargetIdentityMatchesScopeOrFailsClosed` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/consent/target-identity` per-provider account-signal reader, explicit-confirm affordance, mid-session-switch watcher, fail-closed-to-copy/paste routing.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/consent/target-identity`, adjacent to the E4-S2 firewall. FR-CF2 governs which memory is selected; FR-CF7 governs which identity receives it — the firewall's outward half. E4-S2 deliberately reads no provider content for scope; this reads only the minimal identity signal.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (fail-closed firewall, minimal signal read, no silent cross-identity injection).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-D FR-CF7/CF2, F-A FR-D5; FR-AU2 (scope subject).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2722** (E4-S2 firewall), **ENG-2690** (per-provider identity-signal set + TAG).
- [ ] Blocks: safe scoped injection on multi-account providers, E6-S2 E2E.

## 🎯 Story

As a **user who switches between personal and employer contexts** I want **every delivery to bind to the scope that is currently active in my live Orvex session, resolved at the moment of delivery and never inferred from the provider tab or defaulted — and if my Orvex session has expired to be stopped with a re-auth prompt rather than delivered under a stale scope** so that **the firewall always has a live, correct subject and an expired session never leaks a last-known-scope bundle**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestDeliveryBindsLiveScopeAndFailsClosedOnExpiry` (integration — each delivery resolves the active scope from the live Orvex session; a delivery with an expired/absent session fails closed with a visible re-auth prompt and delivers nothing; no unscoped or last-known-scope bundle is ever sent). *Exact session/scope resolution contract is pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a live Orvex session, When delivery runs, Then the active personal-vs-employer scope is resolved from that session at delivery time, never inferred from the provider tab or defaulted. *Assert: scope source == live session; provider tab not consulted for scope.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-H FR-AU2]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given an expired or absent Orvex session at compose time, When delivery is attempted, Then it fails closed — no delivery, a visible re-auth prompt. *Assert: expired → no injection/copy, re-auth shown.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-H FR-AU2, FR-AU1]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a session lapse, When delivery is blocked, Then no unscoped or last-known-scope bundle is used as a fallback. *Assert: zero last-known-scope delivery.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-H FR-AU2]
- [ ] **AC4 (firewall-subject)** — Given the resolved active scope, When the E4-S2 firewall runs, Then it enforces against exactly this scope. *Assert: firewall subject == live-resolved scope.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-H FR-AU2, F-D FR-CF2]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestDeliveryBindsLiveScopeAndFailsClosedOnExpiry` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/auth/active-scope` live resolution per delivery, expired-session fail-closed + re-auth prompt, wiring the resolved scope into the E4-S2 firewall.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/auth/active-scope`. The PRD names F-H as the firewall's missing subject — E4-S2 assumes a known scope; this establishes it live and fails closed on session lapse. Complements E2-S2 (token storage/refresh) with per-delivery scope resolution.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestDeliveryBindsLiveScopeAndFailsClosedOnExpiry` (integration). Tiers: unit (resolution, expiry branch) + integration (delivery gating, re-auth). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: real session fixtures incl. expired.

## 📏 Guidance

## 🎯 Story

As a **foundation engineer** I want **the component bootstrapped by forking `supermemoryai/supermemory` (WXT/MV3 scaffold + per-provider layout, MIT) with its non-F1 scope pruned, the mem0 composer-write technique and Gemini/Claude/Grok selectors pattern-donated (phone-home stripped), and clean license hygiene** so that **we build on proven MV3 prior art per the build-vs-buy ruling (`IgOjzk034v`) instead of the greenfield floor E1-S1 wrongly assumed**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestForkFoundationPrunedDonatedAndLicenseClean` (integration — the tree derives from the supermemory fork with bookmark/capture + broad-permission scope removed; mem0 selectors/technique are present as mined patterns with every `api.supermemory.ai`/`api.mem0.ai` phone-home stripped (FR-TS2); a license gate confirms retained MIT notices on borrowed files and zero GPL/AGPL sources). *Exact fork commit + prune list are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the fork, When bootstrapped, Then it derives from `supermemoryai/supermemory` (WXT/MV3 scaffold + per-provider layout) with non-F1 scope (bookmark/capture, broad permissions) pruned. *Assert: fork provenance present; pruned modules absent.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-G FR-BA1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given borrowed mem0 patterns, When integrated, Then the composer-write technique + Gemini/Claude/Grok selectors are mined as patterns (not a live dependency) with all vendor phone-home removed. *Assert: no `api.supermemory.ai`/`api.mem0.ai` egress; patterns present.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-G FR-BA2, F-F FR-TS2]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given borrowed files, When the license gate runs, Then each retains its MIT copyright/license notice and no GPL/AGPL source is present. *Assert: MIT notices intact; copyleft scan clean.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-G FR-BA4]
- [ ] **AC4 (no-false-credit)** — Given the net-new pieces (canary, review-pause, GO/DEGRADE split, copy/paste degrade UI, enrichment wiring), When accounted, Then they are marked build-fresh and the fork is not credited with them. *Assert: net-new modules flagged fresh, not fork-derived.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-G FR-BA3]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestForkFoundationPrunedDonatedAndLicenseClean` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: fork import + prune, mem0 pattern extraction with phone-home stripped, license-notice retention + copyleft-exclusion gate, build-fresh provenance ledger; reconcile with E1-S1 (which is re-scoped from greenfield to fork-based).

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: repo root + `scripts/provenance`. This corrects the pre-PRD greenfield assumption in E1-S1 (ENG-2711 Context: 'greenfield floor — no prior code') against FR-BA1's fork ruling. The two hardest pieces (breakage canary, pre-send review pause) have zero OSS prior art and stay build-fresh (FR-BA3).

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestForkFoundationPrunedDonatedAndLicenseClean` (integration + license scan). Tiers: static (license/provenance/egress scan) + integration (fork builds + loads). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: assert against the real forked tree.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§2/4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (adopt proven prior art, license hygiene as a gate, no phone-home, honest build-fresh accounting).

proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **security-conscious installer** I want **the extension to ship a reproducible build — documented, independently re-runnable steps from tagged source to the exact store bundle — and a published plain-language data-flow statement of what leaves the browser and to which Orvex endpoint** so that **the extension-trust cost the canon flags (900k / 8M-user malicious-extension exfiltration incidents) is answered with verifiable evidence, not a promise**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestBuildIsReproducibleAndDataFlowStatementPublished` (CI — an independent re-run from the tagged source produces a byte-equivalent store bundle; a published data-flow statement enumerates every egress and is cross-checked against the FR-TS2 bundle network audit so it cannot understate egress). *Exact reproducibility toolchain + statement location are pinned at ENG-2690; this story is dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the tagged source, When the documented build steps are re-run independently, Then the produced store bundle is byte-equivalent to the released one. *Assert: independent rebuild == release bundle (hash match).* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-F FR-TS5(b); §6 NFR-9]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the data-flow statement, When published, Then it enumerates in plain language what leaves the browser and to which Orvex endpoint. *Assert: statement lists every egress + purpose.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-F FR-TS5(c)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the FR-TS2 bundle network audit, When cross-checked against the statement, Then the statement covers exactly the audited egress — no undisclosed call. *Assert: statement egress set == audited egress set.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-F FR-TS5(c), FR-TS2]
- [ ] **AC4 (drift)** — Given a new egress added in code, When CI runs, Then the reproducibility/data-flow gate fails until the statement is updated. *Assert: undisclosed new egress → RED.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-F FR-TS5]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestBuildIsReproducibleAndDataFlowStatementPublished` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: pinned/deterministic build toolchain + documented steps, rebuild-hash CI gate, `DATA-FLOW.md` statement, statement-vs-network-audit cross-check gate.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `release/reproducible` + `DATA-FLOW.md` + CI. These are the FR-TS5 trust levers the PRD makes atomic and testable; they are independent of the still-open open-source-licensing PO decision (§8). Consumes the FR-TS2 egress audit E4-S3 already runs.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestBuildIsReproducibleAndDataFlowStatementPublished` (CI gate). Tiers: integration (independent rebuild hash) + static (statement/audit cross-check). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: rebuild the real artifact, diff hashes.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (reproducible supply chain, honest data-flow disclosure, gate on drift).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-F FR-TS5/TS2, §6 NFR-9; §8 (open-source posture is a separate PO decision — not a dependency).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2727** (E6-S1 packaging/signing), **ENG-2690** (reproducibility toolchain + statement contract + TAG); consumes E4-S3 egress audit.
- [ ] Blocks: store-listing trust posture, GA sign-off.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **release owner** I want **a self-host/enterprise sideload distribution + signed auto-update channel that works if the Chrome Web Store rejects or removes the injecting build, plus a store-listing framing artifact that presents injection as user-initiated own-session assistance** so that **the existential CWS third-party-ToS-facilitation channel risk (FR-PA5, NFR-8) has a named, buildable fallback rather than a single point of failure**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestSideloadChannelAndListingFramingReady` (integration — a signed self-host/enterprise sideload package installs and auto-updates outside the CWS; the store-listing framing artifact states user-initiated own-session assistance and the FR-PA5 permission justifications; AMO is wired as the second channel). *Exact sideload mechanism + listing copy are gated on the §8 store-distribution PO/architecture decision and pinned at ENG-2690; dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given CWS rejection/takedown, When the fallback engages, Then a signed self-host/enterprise sideload package installs and resolves its auto-update channel outside the store. *Assert: sideload install + update resolve without CWS.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA5(b); §8 store-distribution posture]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the store listing, When prepared, Then it frames injection as user-initiated own-session assistance (user reviews and sends; no automated sending/scraping) with per-permission justification. *Assert: framing + justifications present in listing artifact.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA5(a), F-F FR-TS5(a)]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given Firefox AMO, When packaged, Then it is wired as the named second channel behind the sideload-first fallback. *Assert: AMO channel present as fallback #2.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §5 F-B FR-PA5(b); §8]
- [ ] **AC4 (named-risk)** — Given store rejection is a channel risk (not only latency), When release-planning runs, Then the takedown contingency is exercisable, distinct from the FR-BC7 latency channel. *Assert: takedown drill != selector-latency path.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-8; §8]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestSideloadChannelAndListingFramingReady` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: signed self-host/enterprise sideload package + update manifest, AMO second-channel wiring, store-listing framing/justification artifact.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `release/sideload` + listing artifact. Distinct from E6-S1 (CWS+AMO store packaging) and from FR-BC7 (selector-fix latency): this is the existential takedown contingency. §8 marks the store-distribution **sequencing** decision OPEN (PO/architecture) — flag it at pack review; the sideload channel build is concrete regardless.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestSideloadChannelAndListingFramingReady` (integration). Tiers: integration (sideload install/update) + static (listing framing/justification lint). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: install the real sideload package.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (named fallback not single-point-of-failure, honest store framing, exercisable contingency).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §5 F-B FR-PA5, §6 NFR-8, §8 store-distribution posture (OPEN sequencing).

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2727** (E6-S1 store packaging), **ENG-2690** (sideload mechanism + listing copy + TAG); §8 store-distribution decision (PO/architecture) — must-resolve flag.
- [ ] Blocks: shipping resilience under CWS takedown.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

Build work: signed sideload package + update manifest + listing-framing artifact, named test. proposed_issue project/milestone (B6) and blocked_by ENG-2727 (E6-S1, the primary-channel story this adds a fallback to) + ENG-2690 correct per ledger. proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As a **delivery engineer** I want **client-side instrumentation of the click→visible-in-composer path with a p95 budget gate, decomposed into warm retrieval + the FR-BC1 canary transaction + render** so that **the 'instant-inject feel' is measured and defended, and a regression that makes delivery sluggish is caught by a gate, not a user complaint**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test/gate `TestClickToVisibleLatencyMeetsP95Budget` (integration — the extension emits click→visible timing with the retrieval/canary/render split, and a p95-over-rolling-window gate fails when the budget is exceeded; the budget threshold is read from config, architecture-confirmed). *Exact p95 threshold is architecture-owned ([ASSUMPTION 400ms]); instrumentation contract pinned at ENG-2690; dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a delivery, When it completes, Then the extension emits a click→visible-in-composer timing sample decomposed into retrieval + canary-transaction + render. *Assert: sample carries the three-part split.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-1]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a rolling window of samples, When the p95 gate runs, Then it passes within the configured budget and fails when exceeded. *Assert: over-budget window → RED.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-1]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given the budget threshold, When resolved, Then it is read from config (architecture-confirmed), not hard-coded. *Assert: threshold from config; no baked 400.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-1 ([ASSUMPTION] PO/architecture); §8 OQ-E4]
- [ ] **AC4 (no-PII)** — Given timing telemetry, When emitted, Then it carries no memory content or page text (rides the E4-S3 redactor). *Assert: timing payload PII-clean.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-6; §5 F-D (E4-S3 redaction)]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: `TestClickToVisibleLatencyMeetsP95Budget` (AC1–AC4).
- [ ] GREEN: `src/obs/latency` click→visible instrumentation with the three-part split, config-driven p95 budget gate, redactor reuse.

## 🧠 Context

Tier placement: `src/obs/latency`. The precompute/warm-cache mechanism for the instant feel is architecture-owned (OQ-E4); this story owns the measurement + gate the budget is defended by. Reuses the E4-S3 telemetry redactor.

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the ratified extension canon sweep

## 🧪 Testing

Named DoD `TestClickToVisibleLatencyMeetsP95Budget` (integration). Tiers: unit (p95 computation) + integration (end-to-end timing). CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §5: measure a real delivery, not a stub.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§4/6/7; SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t` (testable NFR budget, config-not-baked threshold, PII-clean telemetry).

## 🔗 References

- PRD `QpVnEF7aEU` §6 NFR-1/NFR-6, §8 OQ-E4; FR-BC1 canary budget.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2718** (E3-S2 delivery path), **ENG-2728** (E6-S2 E2E), **ENG-2690** (instrumentation contract + TAG).
- [ ] Blocks: NFR-1 ship-gate.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

## 🎯 Story

As a **non-technical EU-beachhead user** I want **the extension's popup surfaces (connect, status, consent, breakage, copy-ready) to be i18n-capable and English-first with EU-beachhead languages phaseable** so that **the extension can be localized for the regulated EU beachhead without a rewrite, on the same phasing the GTM sets**.

**Definition of Done:** one named test `TestExtensionSurfacesAreI18nReadyEnglishFirst` (integration — all popup user-facing strings are externalized to a locale catalog, English is the default, a pseudo-locale renders without truncation/hardcoded-string leakage, and no locale order is baked). *Exact locale plumbing is pinned at ENG-2690; locale priority is GTM-owned (§6 NFR-7 [ASSUMPTION]); dispatch-blocked until the contract TAG lands.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given the popup surfaces, When scanned, Then all user-facing strings are externalized to a locale catalog with English as the default. *Assert: zero hardcoded user-facing strings; en default present.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-7]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a pseudo-locale, When rendered, Then every surface displays with no truncation or untranslated hardcoded-string leakage. *Assert: pseudo-locale render clean.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-7]

- [ ] **AC4 (distinct-from-ui)** — Given the ui-service i18n work (ENG-2710), When scoped, Then this covers the extension's own surfaces, not the ui web app. *Assert: extension locale catalog is component-local.* [Source: QpVnEF7aEU §6 NFR-7; §4 (extension is component #17)]

## 🧠 Context
